title: dodecatheon pauciflorum var. cusickii
intro: Dodecatheon pauciflorum var. cusickii, also known as Cusick's shooting star, is a perennial flowering plant native to the western United States and Canada. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Cusick's shooting star include making teas, infusions, and tinctures from the leaves and roots of the plant.
